title: DSDS Guide Definitions
description: Defines documents for long-form, reading-oriented guidance that is not tied to a single component, token, foundation, or pattern. A guide covers getting-started walkthroughs, contribution instructions, tutorials, conceptual overviews, migration guides, and any other narrative documentation a design system needs. A guide document holds identity and metadata; the kind of guide (getting-started, contribution, tutorial, and so on) is carried by the `category` metadata field, keeping the entity flexible enough for any guideline. The `metadata` object carries optional metadata fields. Typed entries in the `documentBlocks` array carry structured docs — narrative sections, procedures, guidelines, and use-cases.

guide.description: A long-form, reading-oriented document: a getting-started walkthrough, a contribution guide, a tutorial, a conceptual overview, a migration guide, or any narrative guidance the system needs. Guides fill the gap left by the artifact-bound entities (component, token, foundation, pattern), which document a single thing rather than a journey or a concept. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds optional metadata fields — use `category` (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration') to classify the guide, and `tags` for finer-grained discovery. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: sections (narrative prose), steps (procedures), guidelines, useCases, imports, accessibility, and content.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a guide.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contributing', 'theming-tutorial', 'migrating-to-v2'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case and unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Getting started', 'Contributing', 'Theming tutorial', 'Migrating to v2').

description.description: What this guide covers, who it is for, and what the reader will accomplish. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Use `category` to classify the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration').

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this guide, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the guide-specific kinds (sections, steps) plus reused kinds (imports for setup instructions, guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display, since a guide reads top to bottom. Express links to other entities and external resources through the `links` metadata field or a section's `links`, not as document block entries.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured documentation for agent (AI/LLM) consumption only — a partitioned space accepting the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation constraints, disambiguation between confusable entities, machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing documentation. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
